frameworks.name: bidimensional signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: two-dimensional SDT representation of decision and confidence evidence

frameworks.name: decision-congruent heuristic
frameworks.one_line: ignores response-incongruent dimension when constructing confidence

frameworks.name: unequal-variance signal detection
frameworks.one_line: internal evidence distributions have unequal variance-covariance structure

frameworks.name: blindsight account
frameworks.one_line: dissociation of objective decision from subjective metacognition

core_question: Can a decision-congruent heuristic that ignores response-incongruent information yield superior metacognitive sensitivity under realistic internal variance structures?
key_assumptions: confidence draws on a bidimensional internal evidence representation
internal evidence has unequal variance and may be statistically dependent
metacognitive accuracy reflects mapping from internal evidence to confidence
manipulations: statistical independence vs dependence between dimensions
heuristic strategies for combining or ignoring an evidence dimension
key_findings: ignoring the response-incongruent dimension yields superior metacognitive accuracy
this advantage emerges specifically under unequal-variance structures
the heuristic predicts dissociations between objective decision and subjective metacognition
interpretations: a simple decision-congruent heuristic exploits variance asymmetries hidden to joint readouts
observed metacognition-decision dissociations and blindsight features may reflect this heuristic
